en.name: Conjugated bilirubin in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Bilirubin is an orange-yellow pigment produced by the normal breakdown of heme, a component of the haemoglobin found in red blood cells [1]. **Conjugated (or "direct") bilirubin** is the fraction that has been processed by the liver: glucuronidated bilirubin is water-soluble and ready for excretion in bile, while delta bilirubin is the fraction covalently bound to albumin that accumulates in prolonged cholestasis [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser on serum or plasma. It is collected in routine biochemistry as part of a liver panel, especially when investigating jaundice or cholestasis. Results are reported either in mass units (**mg/dL**) or in SI units (**µmol/L**, the unit recognised by NPU as the standard for substance concentration of bilirubin [2]). Both units are in active use in practice; the choice varies by lab and by country. **Important — "conjugated" vs "direct".** In LOINC, two distinct terms coexist: - **"Bilirubin.conjugated"** (LOINC component `Bilirubin.glucuronidated`) — the strictly glucuronidated fraction, typically measured by HPLC (high-performance liquid chromatography). - **"Bilirubin.direct"** (LOINC component `Bilirubin.glucuronidated+Bilirubin.albumin bound`) — the fraction that reacts with diazo reagents without an accelerator. This includes both glucuronidated bilirubin **and delta-bilirubin**. This is the routine assay used in most clinical laboratories and is what is usually reported on a liver panel under "direct bilirubin" or, somewhat loosely, "conjugated bilirubin". Both are included in this concept set because, in real-world data, the two terms are used interchangeably and the clinical interpretation is the same. Downstream analyses that need to distinguish the two (e.g. studies restricted to HPLC measurements) can filter on the specific concept. ## Included Concepts The set contains four LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, all measured in serum or plasma. Each measurand (conjugated and direct) has a mass-concentration variant (mg/dL) and a substance-concentration variant (µmol/L). ### Bilirubin.conjugated (HPLC, strictly glucuronidated) - *3019676 / 15152-2 — Bilirubin.conjugated [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in mg/dL. - *3005772 / 29760-6 — Bilirubin.conjugated [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in µmol/L. ### Bilirubin.direct (diazo reaction, conjugated + delta) - *3027597 / 1968-7 — Bilirubin.direct [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in mg/dL. This is the most widely used LOINC code in routine US laboratory practice. - *3028638 / 14629-0 — Bilirubin.direct [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in µmol/L. The standard SI code used in Europe. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows the LOINC property: the mass-concentration concepts use **mg/dL** (concept 8840), the substance-concentration concepts use **µmol/L** (concept 8749). Conversion: 1 mg/dL = 17.1 µmol/L; 1 µmol/L = 0.0585 mg/dL (molar mass of bilirubin ≈ 584.66 g/mol). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the two LOINC groups for conjugated bilirubin in serum or plasma (one for the strictly glucuronidated fraction, one for glucuronidated + albumin-bound) and pulling in all their descendants. This naturally excludes: - **Total bilirubin** (covered by a separate concept set). - **Indirect / unconjugated bilirubin** (covered by a separate concept set). - **Other specimens** such as cord blood, body fluids (peritoneal, pleural, dialysis), and urine — direct/conjugated bilirubin variants exist for these but are out of scope for this set. - **Ratios and qualitative tests** such as `Bilirubin.direct/Bilirubin.total`, `Bilirubin.conjugated+indirect`, or `Bilirubin.direct [Presence]`. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "direct bilirubin" or "conjugated bilirubin" without further specification (typical of routine biochemistry analysers): `3027597 / 1968-7 — Bilirubin.direct [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma` if the value is in mg/dL, `3028638 / 14629-0 — Bilirubin.direct [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma` if the value is in µmol/L. Routine clinical assays use the diazo method, which corresponds to the `Bilirubin.direct` concepts even when the source labels them "conjugated". - **Source labelled "HPLC" or explicitly "glucuronidated"**: prefer `3019676 / 15152-2 — Bilirubin.conjugated [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma` (mg/dL) or `3005772 / 29760-6 — Bilirubin.conjugated [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma` (µmol/L). These are appropriate when the lab uses an HPLC method that resolves the strictly glucuronidated fraction. - **Do not confuse with**: total bilirubin, indirect/unconjugated bilirubin, neonatal transcutaneous bilirubin, delta bilirubin (when measured separately), bilirubin in urine, or bilirubin in cord blood — all belong to other concept sets. - **Method is not part of the mapping target**: when the source records the assay method (Jendrassik–Grof, vanadate, dry chemistry, HPLC, etc.), preserve it in the measurement source value but do not let it change the target concept beyond the "direct vs conjugated" choice above. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1968-7 — Bilirubin.direct [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (Part description for "Bilirubin.glucuronidated+Bilirubin.albumin bound"). Available: https://loinc.org/1968-7/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
